crime: She read every prisoner — a guarded manner, a pause before an answer — and what she could not hold in her head she kept in a small book beneath her bunk.
A fellow prisoner searched her bunk, found the book, and read it aloud in the lounge — her watching of each of them opened for the whole room.
That prisoner kept a journal of her own, with one line struck to black — the one secret the girl had told no one.

She raised the struck-out line, whole and legible again, and read it: a hoard of sleeping powder behind a loose stone in the girl's cell, and the one spoonful the girl stirred into her water each night.

In the evening free time she let herself into the girl's solo cell — an entry no one in the corridor thought twice about — and swapped the hoard for a far stronger powder from the medical room, the same to the eye.
That night, locked in alone, the girl measured her usual spoonful into her water and did not wake.

At the morning unlock the girl was found dead of a medicine no one knew she took. In her journal the cancellation still crossed the line, yet the words beneath it read clear. She could have torn the page or re-inked the strike to erase her only trace; she let the sealed line stand instead.

Struck-out ink does not surface on its own, which means someone brought the line back — and the only prisoner who can do that is her. The swap also needed someone inside the girl's solo cell that evening, and a prisoner recalled her letting herself into it in the free-time hour.
fields.epitaph: She proved no page stays sealed, and was read to the last line.
execution: The execution stand rose bearing a long iron bed, a frame of fine styluses suspended above it.
She was laid face-up on the bed and bound, and the frame lowered until its points met her skin.
The styluses wrote — the very lines she had struck from her book, the pages she had kept from every eye — cut legible for the room to read.
The frame worked down her, line by line, and did not lift.
It writes until no skin is left unmarked.

magic.name: The Resurfacing
magic.text: She can make any writing a surface once carried rise back onto that surface, whole and legible, however it was erased, struck out, or torn away.
